reviews.review_msg: Great professor and genuinely a nice guy overall. This class was fun and engaging, with two 'big' assignments (one of them being a group project). Special shoutout to Sage, great TA!
Chris is a great prof. But the 201W course (curriculum and context) itself was dry.

Chris is one of the best profs I've had at SFU. He is so funny and does his best to make lectures entertaining despite its dry content. There are quite a few things to do (quizzes, 3 assignments, labs, tutorial) but the grading scheme is fair and if you put in the work you will succeed! Love Chris!!!!
Chris is one of the best profs for CMNS classes!! He's easy-going, straightforward, and his lectures are easy to follow. Would definitely recommend taking his classes.

Chris is one of the best profs for cmns! His lectures can be quite dry at times but his personality is silly lol. Graded by three easy assignments, quizzes that are simple (just re read slides), and writing exercises in person at the end of class. 10/10 recommend :D

One of the most detailed, clear and caring profs at the entire university. He actually wants you to succeed and even better, wants to help you do it. Do not expect a prof who throws out a guideline and calls it a day. He walks you through what you need to know so specifically that it makes it feel easy to succeed.

Great prof! If you attend lectures and study what is on the slides, you will do fine on the tests. Assignments are marked by your TA (or at least mine were), so how tough stuff is graded really depends on them. Overall, it was a very straightforward and relatively easy course.

Chris is one of my favourite CMNS prof! Super nice and genuinely cares for his students. This was such a fun class because we got to use some really cool equipments and actually take what we learn and apply it. Would definitely take his classes again!

Chris is a great professor who really cares about his students. He seemed very knowledgeable about what he taught and explained his concepts in lecture really well. For the quizzez, if you attend the lecture he usually hinted at what would come, and usually it would be there on the quiz.

He is such a great prof! It's clear he is passionate and wants his students to learn. He is willing to answer any and all questions and teaches the material in a fun way. I actually enjoyed the lectures even though the material can be dry.
